questions.question: What is the fundamental building block of most modern digital integrated circuits?
What architectural feature allows a processor to execute multiple instructions in a single clock cycle, assuming data dependencies permit?

When designing a custom standard cell library, what critical information is typically extracted from the physical layout of each cell (e.g., an inverter or NAND gate) to allow timing analysis tools to accurately predict its delay under various operating conditions?
In the context of processor memory consistency models, what phenomenon does a 'store buffer' primarily address to improve performance, while requiring careful handling to maintain correctness?
What is the purpose of 'clock tree synthesis' (CTS) in the physical design flow of an Integrated Circuit?
options: To place and route standard cells
To generate a detailed list of design errors
To create a balanced distribution network for the clock signal
To optimize power consumption of sequential elements
To convert RTL to a gate-level netlist
